title: Bus Headway Analysis: Route 1
Bus Headway Analysis: Route 1
text: Start listing stops Route 1 can pick ones care . ’ll see stops listed order Harvard Nubian. station column holds short code used internally; stop_name ’ll use tm_bus_stop_id(). Since bus directions always "outbound" (away downtown) "inbound" (toward downtown), don’t need discovery step directions.

what: Step 2: Pull headways for a single day
title: Bus Headway Analysis: Route 1
text: Let’s look headways MIT @ Mass Ave — busy mid-route stop — directions typical winter weekday. result list individual bus arrivals. Bind single data frame add direction label:
code: date <- "2024-01-16"  # Tuesday  inbound  <- tm_headways(date,                         stop = tm_bus_stop_id("1", "MIT @ Mass Ave", "inbound")) outbound <- tm_headways(date,                         stop = tm_bus_stop_id("1", "MIT @ Mass Ave", "outbound")) headways_df <- bind_rows(   bind_rows(inbound)  |> mutate(direction = "Inbound (→ Nubian)"),   bind_rows(outbound) |> mutate(direction = "Outbound (→ Harvard)") )  glimpse(headways_df)

title: Bus Headway Analysis: Route 1
text: departure timestamp (current_dep_dt) comes back ISO 8601 string (e.g. "2024-01-16T09:30:00"). Convert keep daytime service window.
code: headways_df <- headways_df |>   mutate(     dep_time      = as.POSIXct(current_dep_dt, format = "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S",                                 tz = "America/New_York"),     headway_min   = headway_time_sec / 60,     benchmark_min = benchmark_headway_time_sec / 60,     hour          = as.integer(format(dep_time, "%H"))   ) |>   filter(hour >= 6, hour <= 22)

what: Step 4: Plot headways over the day
title: Bus Headway Analysis: Route 1
text: look : Peaks headway mean longer waits buses — often peak (inbound demand) PM peak (outbound demand). headway 15 minutes route ’s supposed run every 8 minutes suggests gap service.
code: ggplot(headways_df, aes(x = dep_time, y = headway_min, color = direction)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 1.2) +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(     "Inbound (→ Nubian)"    = "#003DA5",     "Outbound (→ Harvard)"  = "#80A8DD"   )) +   labs(     title    = "Route 1 Headways at MIT @ Mass Ave",     subtitle = paste("January 16, 2024 ·", date),     x        = NULL,     y        = "Minutes between buses",     color    = NULL   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "top")

what: Step 5: Compare actual vs. scheduled headway
title: Bus Headway Analysis: Route 1
text: benchmark_headway_time_sec field schedule promised. Comparing actual headway shows route running behind schedule. Red bars moments actual gap buses exceeded scheduled gap. Clusters red bars often indicate bus bunching — common problem long surface routes small delays compound.
code: headways_df |>   filter(direction == "Inbound (→ Nubian)") |>   mutate(     excess_min = headway_min - benchmark_min   ) |>   ggplot(aes(x = dep_time, y = excess_min)) +   geom_col(aes(fill = excess_min > 0), width = 60) +     scale_fill_manual(       values = c("FALSE" = "#80A8DD", "TRUE" = "#DA291C"),       labels = c("Under schedule", "Over schedule"),       name   = NULL     ) +     geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.4) +     labs(       title    = "Route 1 Inbound — Excess Wait vs. Schedule",       subtitle = "MIT @ Mass Ave · January 16, 2024",       x        = NULL,       y        = "Extra minutes beyond scheduled headway"     ) +     theme_minimal() +     theme(legend.position = "top")

title: Bus Headway Analysis: Route 1
text: broader view, use tm_aggregate_headways(), returns daily averages rather individual events.
code: agg_raw <- tm_aggregate_headways(   stop       = tm_bus_stop_id("1", "MIT @ Mass Ave", "inbound"),   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31" )  agg_df <- bind_rows(agg_raw) |>   mutate(date = as.Date(service_date)) ggplot(agg_df, aes(x = date, y = mean / 60)) +   geom_line(color = "#003DA5") +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, linetype = "dashed",               color = "grey50") +   labs(     title    = "Route 1 Average Inbound Headway — January 2024",     subtitle = "MIT @ Mass Ave",     x        = NULL,     y        = "Average headway (minutes)"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Bus Headway Analysis: Route 1
text: Now can look stops name, try exploring routes stops: See Finding Stops Name vignette full lookup API across transit modes, including commuter rail ferry.
code: # Which bus routes are available? tm_bus_routes()  # Stops on Route 66 (Harvard → Dudley) tm_bus_stations("66")  # Headways at Nubian on Route 1 — the southern terminus tm_headways("2024-01-16",   stop = tm_bus_stop_id("1", "Nubian Station", "inbound") )

title: Finding Stops by Name
text: five rapid transit lines Red, Orange, Blue, Green, Mattapan.

what: Discover: what stations are on this line?
title: Finding Stops by Name
code: library(transitmattr) tm_line_stations("Red") #>                stop_name     station order branches #> 1                Alewife place-alfcl     1     A, B #> 2                  Davis place-davis     2     A, B #> 3                 Porter place-portr     3     A, B #> 4                Harvard place-harsq     4     A, B #> 5                Central place-cntsq     5     A, B #> 6            Kendall/MIT place-knncl     6     A, B #> 7            Charles/MGH place-chmnl     7     A, B #> 8            Park Street place-pktrm     8     A, B #> 9      Downtown Crossing place-dwnxg     9     A, B #> 10         South Station place-sstat    10     A, B #> 11              Broadway place-brdwy    11     A, B #> 12                Andrew place-andrw    12     A, B #> 13   JFK/UMass (Ashmont)   place-jfk   101        A #> 14            Savin Hill place-shmnl   102        A #> 15         Fields Corner place-fldcr   103        A #> 16               Shawmut place-smmnl   104        A #> 17               Ashmont place-asmnl   105        A #> 18 JFK/UMass (Braintree)   place-jfk   201        B #> 19          North Quincy place-nqncy   202        B #> 20             Wollaston place-wlsta   203        B #> 21         Quincy Center place-qnctr   204        B #> 22          Quincy Adams place-qamnl   205        B #> 23             Braintree place-brntn   206        B

what: Discover: which directions does this line use?
title: Finding Stops by Name
text: Rapid transit lines use geographic direction names rather inbound/outbound.
code: tm_line_directions("Red")   # northbound / southbound #>            0            1  #> "northbound" "southbound" tm_line_directions("Green") # eastbound  / westbound #>           0           1  #> "eastbound" "westbound"

title: Finding Stops by Name
text: can also use 0/1 instead direction names prefer:
code: # Davis, trains heading toward Alewife (northbound) tm_stop_id("Red", "Davis", "northbound") #> [1] "70064"  # Alewife is a terminus — same stop ID in both directions tm_stop_id("Red", "Alewife", "northbound") #> [1] "70061" tm_stop_id("Red", "Alewife", "southbound") #> [1] "70061" tm_stop_id("Red", "Davis", 0)  # same as "northbound" #> [1] "70064" tm_stop_id("Red", "Davis", 1)  # same as "southbound" #> [1] "70063"

title: Finding Stops by Name
text: aggregate endpoints (like tm_aggregate_travel_times()) take place ID instead stop ID. Place IDs refer whole station, platform.

title: Finding Stops by Name
code: # Headways at Davis, trains heading toward Alewife tm_headways("2024-01-15",   stop = tm_stop_id("Red", "Davis", "northbound") )  # Travel time: Park Street → Davis (aggregate endpoint uses place IDs) tm_aggregate_travel_times2(   from_stop  = tm_place_id("Red", "Park Street"),   to_stop    = tm_place_id("Red", "Davis"),   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31" )

title: Finding Stops by Name
text: 121 bus routes constants. Directions always "outbound" "inbound".

title: Finding Stops by Name

what: Discover: what stops are on this route?
title: Finding Stops by Name
code: # Route 1 runs between Harvard and Nubian tm_bus_stations("1") #>                    stop_name station order #> 1                    Harvard   hhgat     1 #> 2      Mass Ave & Putnam Ave   maput     2 #> 3 Central Square (Cambridge)   cntsq     3 #> 4             MIT @ Mass Ave     mit     4 #> 5              Hynes Station   hynes     5 #> 6     Mass Ave (Orange Line)   masta     6 #> 7      Mass Ave @ Washington   wasma     7 #> 8   Melnea Cass @ Washington   melwa     8 #> 9             Nubian Station    nubn     9

title: Finding Stops by Name
text: Bus stop IDs encode route direction — "1-1-110" means route 1, direction 1 (inbound), stop sequence 110.
code: # Harvard, boarding a Route 1 toward Nubian (inbound) tm_bus_stop_id("1", "Harvard", "inbound") #> [1] "1-1-110"  # Harvard, boarding a Route 1 toward Watertown (outbound) tm_bus_stop_id("1", "Harvard", "outbound") #> [1] "1-0-110"

title: Finding Stops by Name
code: # Headways at Harvard on Route 1, inbound, on a specific day harvard_headways = tm_headways("2024-01-15",   stop = tm_bus_stop_id("1", "Harvard", "inbound") )  # Aggregate headways at Nubian over a month aggregate_headways_1 = tm_aggregate_headways(   stop       = tm_bus_stop_id("1", "Nubian Station", "inbound"),   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31" )

title: Finding Stops by Name
text: Commuter Rail routes identified GTFS route ID. 13 lines available.

title: Finding Stops by Name

what: Discover: what stops are on this line?
title: Finding Stops by Name
text: terminus column flags endpoints line.
code: tm_cr_stations("CR-Fairmount") #>             stop_name       station order terminus #> 1           Readville place-DB-0095     1     TRUE #> 2           Fairmount place-DB-2205     2    FALSE #> 3    Blue Hill Avenue place-DB-2222     3    FALSE #> 4       Morton Street place-DB-2230     4    FALSE #> 5       Talbot Avenue place-DB-2240     5    FALSE #> 6 Four Corners/Geneva place-DB-2249     6    FALSE #> 7       Uphams Corner place-DB-2258     7    FALSE #> 8           Newmarket place-DB-2265     8    FALSE #> 9       South Station   place-sstat     9     TRUE

title: Finding Stops by Name
text: CR stations can multiple platform stop IDs station. terminus stations, one direction may stops (trains depart, arrive, direction).
code: # Fairmount station, outbound (toward Readville) tm_cr_stop_id("CR-Fairmount", "Fairmount", "outbound") #> [1] "CR-Fairmount_0_DB-2205-01" "CR-Fairmount_0_DB-2205-02"  # South Station, inbound — terminus, so no inbound stops tm_cr_stop_id("CR-Fairmount", "South Station", "inbound") #> character(0)  # South Station, outbound — all platforms tm_cr_stop_id("CR-Fairmount", "South Station", "outbound") #> [1] "CR-Fairmount_0_NEC-2287"    "CR-Fairmount_0_NEC-2287-01" #> [3] "CR-Fairmount_0_NEC-2287-07" "CR-Fairmount_0_NEC-2287-08" #> [5] "CR-Fairmount_0_NEC-2287-09" "CR-Fairmount_0_NEC-2287-10" #> [7] "CR-Fairmount_0_NEC-2287-11" "CR-Fairmount_0_NEC-2287-12" #> [9] "CR-Fairmount_0_NEC-2287-13"

title: Finding Stops by Name
text: CR stations proper GTFS place IDs (unlike bus ferry). Use tm_aggregate_travel_times().

title: Finding Stops by Name
code: # Headways at Fairmount station, outbound tm_headways("2024-01-15",   stop = tm_cr_stop_id("CR-Fairmount", "Fairmount", "outbound") )  # Aggregate travel time, Fairmount → South Station tm_aggregate_travel_times(   from_stop  = tm_cr_place_id("CR-Fairmount", "Fairmount"),   to_stop    = tm_cr_place_id("CR-Fairmount", "South Station"),   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31" )

title: Finding Stops by Name
text: Six ferry routes available. Like bus CR, directions "outbound" "inbound".

title: Finding Stops by Name

what: Discover: what stops are on this route?
title: Finding Stops by Name
code: # F1: Long Wharf ↔ Hingham tm_ferry_stations("Boat-F1") #>                      stop_name      station order terminus #> 1           Long Wharf (North)    Boat-Long     6     TRUE #> 2                  Rowes Wharf   Boat-Rowes     5    FALSE #> 3 Logan Airport Ferry Terminal   Boat-Logan     4    FALSE #> 4               Georges Island  Boat-George     3    FALSE #> 5                         Hull    Boat-Hull     2    FALSE #> 6                      Hingham Boat-Hingham     1     TRUE

title: Finding Stops by Name
code: # Hingham, boarding a ferry to Long Wharf (inbound) tm_ferry_stop_id("Boat-F1", "Hingham", "inbound") #> [1] "Boat-F1|1|Boat-Hingham"  # Long Wharf (North), boarding a ferry to Hingham (outbound) tm_ferry_stop_id("Boat-F1", "Long Wharf (North)", "outbound") #> [1] "Boat-F1|0|Boat-Long"

title: Finding Stops by Name
code: # Headways at Hingham, inbound ferry_headways = tm_headways("2024-06-01",   stop = tm_ferry_stop_id("Boat-F1", "Hingham", "inbound") )

title: Finding Stops by Name
text: stop-name arguments case-insensitive. Line route arguments also case-insensitive ("red" "Red" work).

title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: transitmattr lets pull live historical data TransitMatters Data Dashboard API directly R. can get things like: routes stops available often trains coming (headways) long trains sit stations (dwell times) long takes travel stops (travel times) many people riding (ridership) slow zones affecting service

title: Getting Started with transitmattr
code: # install.packages("pak")  # run this once if you don't have pak pak::pak("transitmatters/transitmattr")

title: Getting Started with transitmattr
code: library(transitmattr) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(ggplot2)

what: Step 1: Check that the API is working
title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: anything else, ’s good practice confirm API . ’ll get back something like: see "pass" ’re good go.

what: Step 2: Understanding what you get back
title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: tm_* functions return list — R’s way bundling different pieces data together. dated endpoints return unnamed list event records, one per observation (e.g. one per train departure). Use str() see shape [[1]] inspect single record.
code: result <- tm_headways("2024-01-15", stop = "70061")  length(result)    # number of events str(result[[1]])  # fields: route_id, direction, current_dep_dt,                   #   headway_time_sec, benchmark_headway_time_sec,                   #   vehicle_label, vehicle_consist

title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: pulling event data often need know routes stop IDs exist.
code: # All available routes, grouped by mode routes <- tm_routes() names(routes)  # "rapid_transit" "bus" "commuter_rail" "ferry"  # Stop IDs for a specific route (needed for filtering headways, travel times, etc.) # Route IDs come from tm_routes() — e.g. "Red", "Orange", "Green-B" red_stops <- tm_stops("Red")

what: Step 4: Pull data for a single day
title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: functions need date. can also pass stop IDs narrow results — without get every stop, can large response.
code: # Headways at one stop (or a vector of stops) headways <- tm_headways("2024-01-15", stop = "70061") headways <- tm_headways("2024-01-15", stop = c("70061", "70063"))  # Dwell times at a stop dwells <- tm_dwells("2024-01-15", stop = "70063")  # Travel times between a stop pair travel <- tm_travel_times("2024-01-15", from_stop = "70076", to_stop = "70064")  # Alerts for a specific day and route alerts <- tm_alerts("2024-01-15", route = "Red")  # Current live alerts for a route alerts_now <- tm_alerts(route = "Red")

what: Step 5: Pull data over a date range
title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: analysis requires looking range dates. functions take start_date end_date plus extra options. Available line IDs:
code: # Daily Red Line ridership for January 2024 ridership <- tm_ridership(   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31",   line_id    = "Red" )  # Daily trip metrics for the Red Line trips <- tm_trip_metrics(   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31",   agg        = "daily",   line       = "Red" )  # How many trips were scheduled to run each day scheduled <- tm_scheduled_service(   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31",   route_id    = "Red",   agg        = "daily" )  # Total revenue service hours each day on the Red Line hours <- tm_service_hours(   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31",   agg        = "daily",   line_id    = "Red" )

what: Step 8: Turn the result into a data frame
title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: API gives back list, R analysis tools (like ggplot2) prefer data frame. ’s easiest way convert: Tip: bind_rows() doesn’t work, try .call(rbind, lapply(ridership_raw, .data.frame)) — ’s bit verbose handles tricky list shapes.
code: library(dplyr) library(transitmattr)  # Pull ridership data ridership_raw <- tm_ridership("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31", line_id = "Red")  # Each item in ridership_raw is one record — bind them into rows ridership_df <- bind_rows(ridership_raw)  # Now it looks like a spreadsheet head(ridership_df)

title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: data frame can plot ggplot2:
code: library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(transitmattr)  ridership_df <- bind_rows(   tm_ridership("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31", line_id = "Red") )  ggplot(ridership_df, aes(x = as.Date(date), y = count)) +   geom_line(color = "#DA291C") +   # Red Line color   labs(     title = "Red Line Daily Ridership — January 2024",     x     = "Date",     y     = "Riders"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: aggregate endpoints return long-run averages rather individual trip events — useful trend analysis weeks months. Tip: v2 travel times endpoint (tm_aggregate_travel_times2) used Red Line Analysis vignette worked plotting example.
code: # Average travel time from Park Street to Davis, by date travel_agg <- tm_aggregate_travel_times(   from_stop  = "70076",   to_stop    = "70064",   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31" )  # Same data via the v2 endpoint (returns by_date and by_time breakdowns) travel_agg2 <- tm_aggregate_travel_times2(   from_stop  = "70076",   to_stop    = "70064",   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31" )  # Average headways at a stop over a date range headways_agg <- tm_aggregate_headways(   stop       = "70061",   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31" )  # Average dwell times at a stop over a date range dwells_agg <- tm_aggregate_dwells(   stop       = "70063",   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31" )

what: Recap: the functions at a glance
title: Getting Started with transitmattr
text: Next, check Red Line Analysis vignette full worked example goes raw API data polished chart.

title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
text: New packages? dplyr cleaning reshaping data; ggplot2 charts. Install install.packages(c("dplyr", "ggplot2")) needed.
code: library(transitmattr) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(ggplot2)

title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
text: ridership_raw list item represents one day’s record. Let’s flatten data frame:
code: ridership_raw <- tm_ridership(   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31",   line_id    = "Red" ) library(dplyr) ridership_df <- bind_rows(ridership_raw) head(ridership_df) #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   date       count #>   <chr>      <int> #> 1 2024-01-01 72986 #> 2 2024-01-08 78348 #> 3 2024-01-15 79336 #> 4 2024-01-22 84922 #> 5 2024-01-29 88096

title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
text: notice: Weekends typically show lower ridership. day unusually low weekday, may service disruption — check tm_alerts() date.
code: library(ggplot2) ggplot(ridership_df, aes(x = as.Date(date), y = count)) +   geom_col(fill = "#DA291C") +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +   labs(     title = "Red Line Daily Ridership",     subtitle = "January 2024",     x = NULL,     y = "Riders"   ) +   theme_minimal()

what: 2. Travel time from Park Street to Davis
title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
text: uses aggregate endpoint, gives long-run averages rather individual trips.

previous_headings: 2. Travel time from Park Street to Davis
title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
text: Travel time endpoints need platform stop IDs, parent station IDs. station separate IDs direction. Use tm_stops() look : Tip: Northbound (toward Alewife) stop IDs even numbers; southbound (toward Ashmont/Braintree) odd. example, Park Street northbound 70076 southbound 70075.
code: red_stops <- tm_stops("Red")  # Stations are in red_stops$stations — explore one str(red_stops$stations[[1]])

previous_headings: 2. Travel time from Park Street to Davis
title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
code: # Northbound: Park Street (70076) → Davis (70064) travel_raw <- tm_aggregate_travel_times2(   from_stop  = "70076",   to_stop    = "70064",   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31" )

previous_headings: 2. Travel time from Park Street to Davis
title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
code: library(dplyr) travel_df <- bind_rows(travel_raw$by_date)  # Travel times come back in seconds — convert to minutes travel_df <- travel_df |>   mutate(     date       = as.Date(service_date),     travel_min = mean / 60   )  head(travel_df) #> # A tibble: 6 × 15 #>   `25%` `50%` `75%` count holiday   max  mean   min peak  service_date   std #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <int> <lgl>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr> <chr>        <dbl> #> 1 1235. 1270. 1334.   112 TRUE     2011 1298.  1132 all   2024-01-01   108.  #> 2 1251  1302  1362    133 FALSE    2234 1326.  1178 all   2024-01-02   128.  #> 3 1237. 1294. 1356    132 FALSE    1572 1305.  1172 all   2024-01-03    82.6 #> 4 1257. 1308. 1370.   130 FALSE    2674 1336.  1178 all   2024-01-04   156.  #> 5 1241  1288  1344.   131 FALSE    2305 1318.  1167 all   2024-01-05   135.  #> 6 1230. 1304. 1379    116 FALSE    1893 1337.  1134 all   2024-01-06   142.  #> # ℹ 4 more variables: sum <dbl>, weekend <lgl>, date <date>, travel_min <dbl>

previous_headings: 2. Travel time from Park Street to Davis
title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
text: Dashed line smooth trend. travel times creep time can signal slow zones accumulating — leads us section 3.
code: library(ggplot2) ggplot(travel_df, aes(x = date, y = travel_min)) +   geom_line(color = "#DA291C") +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, linetype = "dashed",               color = "grey40") +   labs(     title    = "Average Travel Time: Park Street → Davis",     subtitle = "January 2024 · Red Line",     x        = NULL,     y        = "Minutes"   ) +   theme_minimal() #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

what: 3. Slow zones on a specific day
title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
text: Speed restrictions (“slow zones”) places trains must go slower normal, usually track issues.  Note: slow_raw$available FALSE, active slow zones day. Try different date check period known track work.
code: slow_raw <- tm_speed_restrictions("Red", "2024-01-15") library(dplyr) if (!isTRUE(slow_raw$available)) {   message("No slow zone data available for this date.") } else {   slow_df <- bind_rows(slow_raw$zones)   glimpse(slow_df) } #> Rows: 60 #> Columns: 11 #> $ date        <chr> "2024-01-15", "2024-01-15", "2024-01-15", "2024-01-15", "2… #> $ trackFeet   <int> 699, 412, 599, 500, 300, 86, 475, 99, 513, 100, 100, 800, … #> $ reason      <chr> "Track", "Track", "Track", "Track", "Track", "Track", "Tra… #> $ speedMph    <int> 10, 10, 25, 10, 10, 10, 10, 25, 10, 10, 25, 25, 25, 25, 25… #> $ fromStopId  <chr> "place-pktrm", "place-andrw", "place-chmnl", "place-chmnl"… #> $ reported    <chr> "2023-08-25", "2023-08-01", "2023-08-02", "2023-10-19", "2… #> $ lineId      <chr> "line-Red", "line-Red", "line-Red", "line-Red", "line-Red"… #> $ description <chr> "SB Park to Downtown Cross.", "SB Andrew to JFK", "SB Char… #> $ id          <chr> "572027", "565480", "567089", "000108R", "000108R", "50852… #> $ toStopId    <chr> "place-dwnxg", "place-jfk", "place-pktrm", "place-knncl", … #> $ direction   <chr> "SB", "SB", "SB", "NB", "NB", "NB", "NB", "NB", "NB", "SB"… library(ggplot2) # Each row is a slow zone segment; visualize by location and speed limit ggplot(slow_df, aes(x = reorder(description, speedMph), y = speedMph, fill = speedMph)) +   geom_col() +   coord_flip() +   scale_fill_gradient(low = "#DA291C", high = "#f9c0c0") +   labs(     title    = "Red Line Slow Zones — January 15, 2024",     subtitle = "Lower mph = more restricted",     x        = NULL,     y        = "Speed limit (mph)"   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Red Line Analysis: A Worked Example
text: ’s pattern ’ll use : pull → bind_rows → mutate → ggplot. ’re comfortable , try exploring: tm_routes() — see available lines picking one tm_stops("Orange") — look stop IDs different line tm_aggregate_headways() — often trains come, time tm_line_delays() — delay minutes caused alerts tm_trip_metrics() — -time performance per trip

title: Authors and Citation
text: Andrew Kouré. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Kouré (2026). transitmattr: R Client TransitMatters Data Dashboard API. R package version 0.1.0, https://transitmatters.github.io/transitmattr/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {transitmattr: R Client for the TransitMatters Data Dashboard API},   author = {Andrew Kouré},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://transitmatters.github.io/transitmattr/}, }

what: transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
title: transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
text: R package TransitMatters Data Dashboard API, providing access MBTA performance metrics: headways, dwell times, travel times, ridership, speed restrictions, .

title: transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
text: remotes:

title: transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
code: # Check API health tm_healthcheck()  # List MBTA facilities tm_facilities()  # Service ridership dashboard summary tm_service_ridership_dashboard()

title: transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
text: Functions accept either Date object "YYYY-MM-DD" string.
code: tm_headways("2024-01-15") tm_dwells(as.Date("2024-01-15")) tm_travel_times("2024-01-15")  # Dated or current alerts tm_alerts() tm_alerts("2024-01-15")

title: transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
code: tm_aggregate_travel_times(   from_stop  = "place-pktrm",   to_stop    = "place-davis",   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31" )  tm_aggregate_headways(   stop       = "place-davis",   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-01-31" )

title: transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
code: # Ridership for the Red Line tm_ridership("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31", line_id = "Red")  # Delay summary by line tm_line_delays("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31", line = "Red")  # Trip metrics tm_trip_metrics("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31", agg = "daily", line = "Red")  # Scheduled service (optionally filtered to one route) tm_scheduled_service("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31", agg = "daily") tm_scheduled_service("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31", agg = "daily", route_id = "Red")  # Speed restrictions / slow zones tm_speed_restrictions("Red", "2024-01-15")  # Service hours tm_service_hours("2024-01-01", "2024-01-31", agg = "daily")

title: transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API
transitmattr — R Client for the TransitMatters API

Get MBTA service alerts — tm_alerts
text: user_date Date object, "YYYY-MM-DD" string, NULL (default) current alerts. route One MBTA route IDs (e.g. "Red", "Orange"). Required. Use tm_routes() list valid IDs. base_url Base URL TransitMatters API. Defaults getOption("tm_dashboard_base_url") production host.

title: Get delay data by line — tm_line_delays
text: start_date Start date range. Date object "YYYY-MM-DD" string. end_date End date range. Date object "YYYY-MM-DD" string. line MBTA line identifier, e.g. "Red". agg Aggregation level: "daily" "weekly". Defaults "weekly" NULL. base_url Base URL TransitMatters API. Defaults getOption("tm_dashboard_base_url") production host.

what: Look up the GTFS place ID for a rapid transit station — tm_place_id
title: Look up the GTFS place ID for a rapid transit station — tm_place_id
text: Returns GTFS place identifier used aggregate travel-time endpoints (e.g. "place-alfcl", "place-davis").

title: Look up the GTFS place ID for a rapid transit station — tm_place_id
Look up the GTFS place ID for a rapid transit station — tm_place_id
text: line Line name: "Red", "Orange", "Blue", "Green", "Mattapan" (case-insensitive; "line-red" form also accepted). stop_name Station name (case-insensitive), e.g. "Alewife", "Davis". Use tm_line_stations() see valid names.

title: Look up the GTFS place ID for a rapid transit station — tm_place_id
text: single GTFS place ID string.

what: Look up numeric stop IDs for a rapid transit station — tm_stop_id
title: Look up numeric stop IDs for a rapid transit station — tm_stop_id
text: Returns stop ID(s) used headway, dwell, travel-time endpoints given station direction travel.

title: Look up numeric stop IDs for a rapid transit station — tm_stop_id
Look up numeric stop IDs for a rapid transit station — tm_stop_id
text: line Line name: "Red", "Orange", "Blue", "Green", "Mattapan" (case-insensitive; "line-red" form also accepted). stop_name Station name (case-insensitive), e.g. "Alewife", "Davis". Use tm_line_stations() see valid names. direction Direction travel. Either direction name line (e.g. "northbound", "southbound", "eastbound", "westbound") numeric code 0 1. Use tm_line_directions() see names apply given line.

title: Look up numeric stop IDs for a rapid transit station — tm_stop_id
text: character vector stop IDs (stations one; branching multi-platform stations may several).

title: Look up numeric stop IDs for a rapid transit station — tm_stop_id

what: transitmattr: R Client for the TransitMatters Data Dashboard API — transitmattr-package
title: transitmattr: R Client for the TransitMatters Data Dashboard API — transitmattr-package
text: thin 'httr2'-based wrapper around TransitMatters Data Dashboard API, providing access MBTA performance metrics including headways, dwell times, travel times, ridership, service data.

title: transitmattr: R Client for the TransitMatters Data Dashboard API — transitmattr-package
text: Maintainer: Andrew Kouré akoure@transitmatters.info Authors: Andrew Kouré akoure@transitmatters.info

title: transitmattr 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release.
